title: Reading gauntlet
pedagogy: Mixed review (2/2): applied reading — tone identification on real words combining class, liveness, and marks, interleaved with recognition of rarer characters.

exercises.promptNote: khai — ข high class; live (sonorant ย); "to sell"
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: High class + live + no mark = rising tone. ขาย means "to sell".

exercises.promptNote: pho — พ LOW class + mai ek; "father"
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Low class + mai ek = falling tone. พ่อ means "father".

exercises.promptNote: pak — ป mid class; dead (stop ก); "mouth"
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Mid class + dead = low tone. ปาก means "mouth".

exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: High class + live + no mark = rising tone. เสือ means "tiger".

exercises.promptNote: khit — ค LOW class; dead (stop ด), short vowel; "to think"
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Low class + dead + short vowel = high tone. คิด means "to think".

exercises.promptNote: kaeo — ก mid class + mai tho; "glass"
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Mid class + mai tho = falling tone. แก้ว means "glass".

exercises.promptNote: mue — ม LOW class; live (long ◌ือ); "hand"
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Low class + live + no mark = mid tone. มือ means "hand".
